title: A New Sunscreen Ingredient Wins FDA Approval

source.title: Doctor explains why bemotrizinol, a sunscreen ingredient, took so long to be authorized

segments.title: FDA Approval
segments.audioText: The Food and Drug Administration approved the chemical compound bemotrizinol in sunscreens sold in the U.S.
segments.transcript: The Food and Drug Administration approved the chemical compound bemotrizinol in sunscreens sold in the U.S.
segments.translationZh: 美國食品藥物管理局批准咗化合物 bemotrizinol，可以用喺美國出售嘅防曬產品入面。

points.label: The Food and Drug Administration
points.explanationZh: 即 FDA，全名較長，聽嘅時候可以當成一個機構名 chunk。

points.label: approved the chemical compound
points.explanationZh: approved 係批准；chemical compound 即化合物，新聞健康報道常見。

points.label: in sunscreens sold in the U.S.
points.explanationZh: sold in the U.S. 係過去分詞片語，意思係『喺美國出售嘅』。

segments.title: First Time in Years
segments.audioText: It's the first time in over 25 years that the FDA has approved a new sunscreen ingredient.
segments.transcript: It's the first time in over 25 years that the FDA has approved a new sunscreen ingredient.
segments.translationZh: 呢係超過 25 年以嚟，FDA 第一次批准一種新嘅防曬成分。

points.label: It's the first time in over 25 years
points.explanationZh: 呢個句型用嚟講『隔咗好耐第一次』；in over 25 years 即超過 25 年以嚟。

points.label: that the FDA has approved
points.explanationZh: has approved 用現在完成式，強調到而家為止發生咗嘅新批准。

points.label: a new sunscreen ingredient
points.explanationZh: ingredient 係成分；sunscreen ingredient 即防曬成分。

segments.title: Used Overseas
segments.audioText: Bemotrizinol has been used in Europe and Asia for decades.
segments.transcript: Bemotrizinol has been used in Europe and Asia for decades.
segments.translationZh: Bemotrizinol 已經喺歐洲同亞洲使用咗幾十年。

points.label: has been used
points.explanationZh: 被動語態，意思係『一直被使用』；健康產品報道好常見。

points.label: in Europe and Asia
points.explanationZh: 地區名連讀時，Europe and Asia 中間嘅 and 會講得較輕。

points.label: for decades
points.explanationZh: for decades 即幾十年；for 加時間長度表示持續多久。

segments.title: What It Means
segments.audioText: CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.
segments.transcript: CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.
segments.translationZh: CBS News 醫療記者 Céline Gounder 醫生會帶嚟更多資料。

points.label: medical correspondent
points.explanationZh: correspondent 係記者；medical correspondent 即醫療記者。

points.label: Dr. Céline Gounder
points.explanationZh: 人名唔需要逐字拆，聽到 Dr. 後面接人名就可以。

points.label: has more
points.explanationZh: 新聞口語入面 has more 意思係『有更多報道／資料』。

sentences: The Food and Drug Administration approved the chemical compound bemotrizinol in sunscreens sold in the U.S.
It's the first time in over 25 years that the FDA has approved a new sunscreen ingredient.
Bemotrizinol has been used in Europe and Asia for decades.
CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

vocabulary.example: The agency approved a new ingredient.
vocabulary.translationZh: 該機構批准咗一種新成分。

vocabulary.example: I put on sunscreen before going outside.
This sunscreen has a new ingredient.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢款防曬有一種新成分。

vocabulary.example: The ingredient has been used for decades.
vocabulary.translationZh: 呢種成分已經用咗幾十年。

vocabulary.example: A medical correspondent explained the news.
vocabulary.translationZh: 一位醫療記者解釋咗呢則新聞。

phrases.example: These products are sold in the U.S.

phrases.phrase: the first time in over 25 years
phrases.meaningZh: 超過 25 年以嚟第一次
phrases.example: It's the first time in over 25 years that the rule has changed.
This method has been used for decades.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 跟讀第一句，將 The Food and Drug Administration 當成一個長 chunk，唔好逐字停。
重播 00:08，先聽寫 It's the first time in over 25 years，再補 that the FDA has approved...。
用 the first time in over... 練替換：It's the first time in over a month that I've gone running.
用兩句英文複述：The FDA approved a new sunscreen ingredient. It has already been used in Europe and Asia for decades.

notes: 今日新聞句子有健康同監管用語，重點係聽長機構名、被動語態同時間長度。
先用時間碼聽 00:00、00:08、00:17、00:25；第二輪先睇稿確認 approved、ingredient、decades。
Bemotrizinol 係專有名詞，唔需要背拼法；練習時重點放喺句型同新聞 chunk。
dailyLifePractice 會用較輕鬆嘅日常場景，練買日用品、問店員同改約時間。

dailyLifePractice.title: Asking for Sunscreen in a Store
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你喺藥房或超市想買防曬，要問店員邊度有同有冇適合日常用嘅款。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Excuse me, where can I find sunscreen? I'm looking for something light for everyday use. Do you have any recommendations?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Excuse me, where can I find sunscreen?
lines.zh: 唔好意思，防曬喺邊度可以搵到？

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.en: It's in aisle five, near the skincare products.
lines.zh: 喺第五條通道，護膚品附近。

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I'm looking for something light for everyday use.
lines.zh: 我想搵一款日常用、質地輕身啲嘅。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: something light for everyday use 好自然，唔需要講得太專業。Do you have any recommendations? 係美國店舖入面好常用又有禮貌嘅問法。

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 sunscreen 換成 moisturizer、lip balm 或 hand lotion。

drills.label: Quick repeat
drills.prompt: 跟讀 I'm looking for something light for everyday use 三次。
drills.example: I'm looking for something light for everyday use.

dailyLifePractice.title: Checking a Product Label
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你同朋友一齊買日用品，想確認標籤同成分，但唔想講到好正式。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Let me check the label real quick. I want to see what's in it. This one looks pretty good.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Let me check the label real quick.
lines.zh: 等我快快睇一睇個標籤。

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: Sure. What are you looking for?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I want to see what's in it.
lines.zh: 我想睇下入面有咩成分。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: real quick 係口語，意思係『好快咁』；This one looks pretty good 比 This product is acceptable 自然好多。

drills.label: Say-it-your-way
drills.prompt: 用 Let me check... real quick 講一個你想快快確認嘅嘢。
drills.example: Let me check the price real quick.

drills.label: Short response
drills.prompt: 朋友問 What are you looking for? 用一句簡短英文答。
drills.example: I want to see what's in it.

dailyLifePractice.title: Pushing Plans Back
dailyLifePractice.contextZh: 場景：你買嘢或做 errands 用多咗時間，要同朋友改約遲少少。
dailyLifePractice.audioText: Can we push lunch back a bit? I'm still running errands. I can meet you around twelve-thirty.

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: Can we push lunch back a bit?
lines.zh: 我哋可唔可以將午餐推遲少少？

lines.speaker: Friend
lines.en: No problem. Are you still at the store?

lines.speaker: You
lines.en: I can meet you around twelve-thirty.
lines.zh: 我大概十二點半可以見你。

dailyLifePractice.nativeNoteZh: push lunch back a bit 係自然又禮貌嘅改約講法。running errands 指處理日常雜務，例如買嘢、寄件、攞藥等。

drills.label: Polite rephrase
drills.prompt: 用 Can we push...back a bit? 改另一個安排。
drills.example: Can we push the call back a bit?

drills.label: Substitution
drills.prompt: 將 twelve-thirty 換成 one、after work 或 tomorrow morning。
drills.example: I can meet you after work.
